description:
The ~/.shosts (in each user's home directory) files list remote hosts and users that are trusted by the local system. To remove these files, run the following command to delete them from any location: 
$ sudo find / -name '.shosts' -type f -delete
rationale:
The .shosts files are used to configure host-based authentication for
individual users or the system via SSH. Host-based authentication is not
sufficient for preventing unauthorized access to the system, as it does not
require interactive identification and authentication of a connection request,
or for the use of two-factor authentication.
ocil:
To verify that there are no .shosts files on the system, run the following command: 
$ sudo find / -name '.shosts'
fixtext:
Remove any found ".shosts" files from the system.

$ sudo rm /[path]/[to]/[file]/.shosts
srg_requirement: There must be no .shosts files on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

stig.srg_requirement: There must be no .shosts files on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.
stig.vuldiscussion: The .shosts files are used to configure host-based authentication for individual users or the system via SSH. Host-based authentication is not sufficient for preventing unauthorized access to the system, as it does not require interactive identification and authentication of a connection request, or for the use of two-factor authentication.
stig.checktext:
Verify there are no ".shosts" files on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 with the following command:

$ sudo find / -name .shosts

If a ".shosts" file is found, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Remove any found ".shosts" files from the system.

$ sudo rm /[path]/[to]/[file]/.shosts

title: Remove User Host-Based Authentication Files
